examples: This chapter examines the "Golden Age" of the Hollywood film studio system and explores how a particular kind of filmmaking developed during this period in US film history.
Hollywood has a laissez-passer to China's first foundry of filmmaking.
But, back in Canada, an attempt at collaborative filmmaking with some friends came to nothing.
Now the pair is shaking up the industry with its game-changing approach to filmmaking: "day-and-date" releases.
Per Batton, operant conditioning plays a hand in game design which filmmaking cannot replicate.
